docs.title: AOP Wiki Data Model with EMOD
docs.text:
This is a LinkML schema for the AOP-Wiki data model, extended with EMOD concepts.

URI: http://example.org/aopwiki-emod

Name: aopwiki-emod

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Class Description Aop AopEvent AopLifeStage AopLog AopRelationship AopSex AopStressor AopTaxon Assay AssayBioTargetFamily AssignedLicense BatchImport BiologicalAction BiologicalObject BiologicalOrganization BiologicalProcess BioTargetFamily CellTerm Chemical ChemicalSynonym Citation CitationBioTargetFamily CitationRelationship ConfidenceLevel Directness Event EventBioTargetFamily EventComponent EventLifeStage EventLog EventSex EventTaxon Evidence ExperimentType Handbook HarmonizedAop HarmonizedEvent License LifeStageTerm Observation ObservationCitation OecdStatus OrganTerm Profile Relationship RelationshipLifeStage RelationshipLog RelationshipSex RelationshipTaxon SexTerm Stressor StressorChemical StressorLog TaxonTerm UrlLink User

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Slot Description abstract active_version affiliation aop_id aops applicability_of_the_aop assay_id assays assigned_license_id authors background batch_code batch_import_id bio_target_families biological_action_id biological_object_id biological_organization_id The level of biological organization at which the Key Event occurs (e biological_plausibility biological_process_id biological_target_family_id casrn cell_term_id The cell type in which the Key Event takes place changed_at Timestamp of when the record was last changed characterization_of_exposure chemical_description chemical_id citation_id citations coached_aops coaches completion_score confidence_id contributed_aops contributor_id contributors corresponding_author_id country created_at Timestamp of when the record was created definition A description of the biological state being observed or measured, the biologi... description detection_technology development_strategy directness_id doi downstream_event_id downstream_observation_id dsstox_chemical_id dsstox_substance_id email empirical_support essentiality_id event_components event_id events evidence_collection_strategy evidence_for_chemical_initiation Documentation of how specific prototypical stressors (generally chemicals) ca... evidence_text examples_using_ao Examples illustrating the regulatory significance of this Key Event when it s... exp_type experimental_design external_assay_id first_name forum_links github_handle graphical_representation_image_uid handbook_id harmonized_event_id harmonized_label harmonized_label_key has_method_text Indicates whether method text has been provided for this Key Event how_it_works The mechanistic basis explaining how perturbation of this Key Event leads to ... id inchi inchi_key indigo_inchi_key integration_score Calculated metric for integration and priority assessment of the Key Event internal_notes is_priority jchem_inchi_key journal key_event_essentiality known_loops known_modulating_factors label last_name legacy license_id life_stage_term_id life_stages link_source logo_uid mailing_address mapping_status mean_event_score mean_ker_score measured_or_detected A description of the type(s) of measurements that can be employed to evaluate... modulating_factors name ncbi_id new_aop_id notes objects observation_id observations oecd_project oecd_status_id official_name optional_considerations orcid organ_term_id The organ or tissue in which the Key Event takes place other overall_assessment overall_assessment_file_uid pdf_copy_uid pdf_only phenotype preferred_name process_is_phenotype processes prototypical_stressor pubchem_compound_id pubchem_substance_id quantitative_considerations quantitative_understanding quantitative_understanding_id raw_mapping_value references relationship_id relationships release_date release_notes release_notes_doc_uid released response_relationship row_order saaop_status_id scientific_term sequence sex_term_id sexes short_code short_name sort source source_aop_id source_event_id source_event_id_text source_event_label source_id stressor_id supporting_tax_evidence Evidence describing the taxonomic applicability of the Key Event, using Latin... tag taxon_evidence taxon_term_id taxon_terms taxons term term_class time_scale time_zone title type uncertainties updated_at Timestamp of when the record was last updated upstream_event_id upstream_observation_id url url_links user_defined_ao docs.title: Types
docs.text: Type Description Boolean A binary (true or false) value Curie a compact URI Date a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar DateOrDatetime Either a date or a datetime Datetime The combination of a date and time Decimal A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal speci... Double A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification Float A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification Integer An integer Jsonpath A string encoding a JSON Path Jsonpointer A string encoding a JSON Pointer Ncname Prefix part of CURIE Nodeidentifier A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model Objectidentifier A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model Sparqlpath A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path String A character string Time A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular... Uri a complete URI Uriorcurie a URI or a CURIE

docs.title: Type: Time
docs.text:
A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular day

URI: xsd:time

docs.title: Type Properties
docs.text: Property Value Base XSDTime Type URI xsd:time Representation str

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• URI is dateTime because OWL reasoners do not work with straight date or time
  • If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "time".

docs.title: Schema Source
docs.text: • from schema: http://example.org/aopwiki-emod

docs.title: Mappings
docs.text: Mapping Type Mapped Value self xsd:time native http://example.org/aopwiki-emod/time exact schema:Time

docs.title: Type: Uri
docs.text:
a complete URI

URI: xsd:anyURI

docs.title: Type Properties
docs.text: Property Value Base URI Type URI xsd:anyURI Representation str

docs.title: Comments
docs.text: • in RDF serializations a slot with range of uri is treated as a literal or type xsd:anyURI unless it is an identifier or a reference to an identifier, in which case it is translated directly to a node

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "uri".

A description of the biological state being observed or measured, the biological compartment in which it is measured, and its general role in the biology.

URI: http://example.org/aopwiki-emod/definition Alias: definition

docs.title: Applicable Classes
docs.text: Name Description Modifies Slot Event no

docs.title: Type and Range
docs.text: Property Value Range String Domain Of Event

docs.title: Cardinality and Requirements
docs.text: Property Value ### Slot Characteristics Property Value Owner Event

name: definition
description: A description of the biological state being observed or measured, the
  biological compartment in which it is measured, and its general role in the biology.
